llama-news.headline: Security Breach at Finance Authority Traced to Misconfigured Document Permissions
llama-news.summary: A major data exposure at the Llamaverse Finance Authority was traced to overly permissive document-level access controls, exposing 1.2 million citizen records.
llama-news.body:
FINANCIAL DISTRICT — A security review commissioned by the Llamaverse Finance Authority has concluded that a data exposure affecting 1.2 million citizen records was caused by a misconfiguration of document-level permissions in the authority's central document store.

Sensitive financial records had been loaded with read permissions granted to the public-facing read role rather than the restricted citizen-services role. Because the organisation had not implemented permission queries — queries that test access rights before returning documents — the exposure went undetected for six weeks.

'The records were findable by any authenticated user,' the review stated. 'There was no technical barrier to access beyond authentication.'

Database administrators familiar with MarkLogic noted that xdmp:document-get-permissions() and collection-scoped permission queries provide exactly the automated verification that could have surfaced the issue at load time. The Finance Authority has committed to implementing permission-query based validation gates on all future ingest pipelines.
